story.iWant: Spring Modulith configured to enforce module boundaries
story.soThat: architectural violations are caught at compile time
story.tasks: - Add Spring Modulith 1.4.4 to version catalog - Add Spring Modulith dependencies to framework modules - Create shared/testing module - Add Konsist 0.17.3 to shared/testing dependencies - Implement ArchitectureTest.kt with Konsist rules - Add architecture tests to CI/CD (./gradlew check) - Run ./gradlew :shared:testing:test - verify tests pass - Generate Spring Modulith module canvas - Document module boundaries in architecture.md - Commit: "Add Spring Modulith and Konsist for boundary enforcement"

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Spring Modulith 1.4.4 dependency added to framework modules
Konsist 0.17.3 architecture tests created in shared/testing module
Tests verify: package-by-feature structure, no cyclic dependencies, hexagonal architecture compliance
Module boundary violations fail the build
All current modules pass boundary validation

doc.title: Architecture Document
doc.snippet: Spring Modulith 1.4.4 provides compile-time module boundary verification with Konsist integration, hexagonal architecture enforcement, and event publication registry. Combines programmatic boundary enforcement with Spring's native modularity.

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.section: FR010 - Hexagonal Architecture with Swappable Adapters
doc.snippet: The system shall implement hexagonal architecture where infrastructure components are swappable adapters isolated from domain logic.

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Konsist 0.17.3 architecture tests verify module boundaries, with zero violations policy. Tests include: no cyclic dependencies, hexagonal compliance, package-by-feature structure. Execution target: under 5 seconds.

doc.title: Coding Standards
doc.snippet: Konsist tests enforce module dependencies, coding standards (no wildcard imports, Kotest-only), security standards (REST security annotations, tenant validation), and multi-tenancy patterns (command handler validation).

doc.title: Test Strategy
doc.snippet: Static analysis layer includes Konsist 0.17.3 for architecture boundary validation. Execution in pre-commit hooks and CI pipeline. Target: 100% compliance (zero violations). konsistTest source set with dedicated test execution.

artifact.reason: Spring Modulith version already defined in catalog. Story will add Spring Modulith dependencies using this version.
Konsist version already defined in catalog. Story will add Konsist dependencies using this version.
Existing testing module where Konsist architecture tests will be added. Already has Kotest and Testcontainers configured.
Example framework module where Spring Modulith dependencies will be added. Uses eaf.testing and eaf.kotlin-common convention plugins.
Directory exists but is currently empty. Konsist ArchitectureTest.kt will be created here.

gradle.library: Spring Modulith test support for module verification
Architecture testing framework for boundary enforcement

constraints.constraint: All framework modules must add Spring Modulith 1.4.4 dependencies (implementation: spring-modulith-api, testImplementation: spring-modulith-test)
Konsist 0.17.3 must be added to shared/testing module for centralized architecture tests
Architecture tests must use Kotest (NOT JUnit) - FreeSpec or FunSpec test styles required
Tests must verify: package-by-feature structure, no cyclic dependencies, hexagonal architecture compliance (domain should not depend on infrastructure/adapter)
Module boundary rules must match architecture.md specifications: framework/core is base layer, other modules may depend on allowed modules only
Konsist tests must be added to ./gradlew check task execution for CI/CD integration
All imports must be explicit (NO wildcard imports) - enforced by ktlint and Konsist
Zero violations policy: Build must fail if any architecture boundary is violated
Spring Modulith module canvas generation required for documentation

tests.standards: Architecture tests MUST use Kotest 6.0.4 with FreeSpec or FunSpec style (JUnit explicitly forbidden). Tests execute via ./gradlew :shared:testing:test and are included in ./gradlew check for CI/CD. Konsist 0.17.3 provides architecture boundary verification with zero violations policy enforced. Test execution target: under 5 seconds. All architecture tests reside in shared/testing/src/test/kotlin/com/axians/eaf/testing/architecture/. Tests must verify module dependencies, coding standards (no wildcard imports, Kotest-only), hexagonal architecture (domain isolation), and security patterns (REST security, tenant validation). Constitutional TDD principle applies: architecture tests created FIRST, then implementation follows.

ideas.idea: Test: Verify Spring Modulith 1.4.4 dependency present in framework module build files (use Konsist to scan build.gradle.kts files for spring-modulith dependencies)
Test: Verify ArchitectureTest.kt exists in shared/testing/src/test/kotlin/com/axians/eaf/testing/architecture/ with Kotest FreeSpec

Test: Verify Konsist tests include: modules().assertDoesNotHaveCircularDependencies(), classes().withPackage("..domain..").shouldNotDependOn("..infrastructure.."), imports.assertNone { it.isWildcard }
Test: Intentionally violate module boundary (add forbidden dependency), verify build fails with clear error message from Konsist
Test: Verify Spring Modulith module canvas generated (check for module documentation artifacts)
Test: Run ./gradlew check, verify architecture tests execute and pass (check test reports for Konsist tests)
Test: Run all Konsist rules against current modules, verify zero violations (all framework modules pass boundary validation)
